title: Data Types
description: Understand the different kinds of data in JavaScript

blocks.content: JavaScript supports several fundamental data types. Each type tells JavaScript how to interpret and work with the data.

**Numbers**: Integer and decimal values
**Strings**: Text values, enclosed in quotes
**Booleans**: `true` or `false` (for yes/no decisions)
**undefined**: A variable that hasn't been assigned a value
**null**: Explicitly means "no value"
Use the `typeof` operator to find out what type a value is.

blocks.code:
console.log(typeof 42);           // "number"
console.log(typeof "hello");      // "string"
console.log(typeof true);          // "boolean"
console.log(typeof undefined);     // "undefined"
console.log(typeof null);          // "object" (quirk of JavaScript!)

blocks.content: Sometimes JavaScript automatically converts data from one type to another (coercion). Sometimes you need to do it manually.
Falsy values in JavaScript: `0`, `""` (empty string), `false`, `null`, `undefined`, and `NaN`. Everything else is truthy.
